content:
Your LLM streams tokens. Plain JSON breaks the experience. Here is the Rails SSE setup tutorials leave out.

9 min read
Just now

--

Press enter or click to view image in full size

Your LLM streams tokens. Plain JSON breaks the experience. Here is the Rails SSE setup tutorials leave out.Your demo looked great. You typed a question, waited two seconds, and the full answer appeared.
Then the frontend team wired it into the real Next.js app. The first user complaint was that it just hangs, then dumps a wall of text.
That is what a rails api streaming AI chat NextJS setup looks like when you skip streaming and render the whole response as one JSON blob. The LLM already streams tokens on the provider side.
Your render json: call throws that away and waits for the last token before sending anything.
I have shipped this wrong twice before I stopped skipping the plumbing. Both times, the fix was the same three pieces.
A Rails controller that streams Server-Sent Events. A service object that turns RubyLLM’s token callback into SSE frames.
A Next.js client that reads the response body incrementally instead of waiting for fetch to resolve.
Write side, the half that emits tokens:
RubyLLM chat.ask block
↓
ActionController::Live
↓
response.stream.write (SSE frame)
↓
nginx…
